message:
[MEDIUM] fcp-protocol: close encode/decode asymmetry on empty source_id in SignedFcpsFrame

SignedFcpsFrame::decode rejects source_id_len == 0 (line 1138 checks
`source_id_len == 0 || bytes.len() < ...` and fails with TooShort), but
validate_source_id — called by both new() and try_encode() — only gated
the upper bound (>u16::MAX). An empty TailscaleNodeId therefore produced
a fully-signed frame that encoded successfully on the sender and then
failed to decode at the receiver. Classic encode/decode roundtrip
asymmetry; on-wire observers of the sender see valid-looking frames
that no peer will accept.

Fix: validate_source_id also rejects empty source_id with a new
FrameError::SourceIdEmpty variant. Now new() and encode() fail fast
with a descriptive error, consistent with decode's rejection.

Tests:
- signed_frame_new_rejects_empty_source_id: SignedFcpsFrame::new("")
  returns SourceIdEmpty at the first validator.
- signed_frame_encode_rejects_mutated_empty_source_id: simulates a
  struct mutated after construction; encode fails closed instead of
  emitting an undecodable frame.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-protocol --lib signed_frame → 17 passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
